listening.title: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
listening.topic: A volunteer diver filmed a rare encounter with an endangered Great White shark in the waters between Tunisia and Sicily.
listening.topic_zh: 一名志願潛水員在突尼西亞與西西里島之間的海域拍攝到與瀕危大白鯊相遇的罕見畫面。
listening.transcript_excerpt: When ocean conservationist and volunteer diver Saverio Minichino descended into the deep blue waters of the Sicilian Channel, he had no idea he was about to come face to face with one of the ocean's most iconic and misunderstood predators — the Great White shark. Shaking with a mixture of fear and awe, he managed to keep his camera steady long enough to capture what scientists are calling extraordinarily rare footage. Great White sharks have been virtually absent from Mediterranean waters for decades, making this sighting not only remarkable but scientifically significant. Marine biologists believe the population of Great Whites in the Mediterranean has declined dramatically due to overfishing, habitat loss, and a severe reduction in their prey species. The shark, estimated to be around four metres in length, appeared calm and moved gracefully through the water before disappearing into the depths. Conservation groups have seized upon the footage as powerful evidence for strengthening legal protections for the species in European waters. While Great Whites are listed as vulnerable on the IUCN Red List, enforcement of protective legislation in the Mediterranean remains inconsistent. Experts hope that viral footage like this will raise public awareness and put pressure on governments to take more decisive action to safeguard these apex predators before it is too late.

vocabulary.definition: An animal at the top of the food chain that is not hunted by any other animal in its natural environment.
vocabulary.definition_zh: 食物鏈頂端的掠食者，在自然環境中不會被其他動物獵殺。
vocabulary.example: Great White sharks are considered apex predators, playing a vital role in maintaining the balance of marine ecosystems.

vocabulary.definition: Describing a species that is at serious risk of extinction due to threats such as habitat destruction or overhunting.
vocabulary.definition_zh: 描述因棲地破壞或過度獵捕等威脅而面臨絕種嚴重風險的物種。
vocabulary.example: The endangered status of the Great White shark has prompted international calls for stronger conservation measures.

vocabulary.definition: Recorded video material, especially of a particular event or subject.
vocabulary.definition_zh: 錄製的影片素材，尤指特定事件或主題的影像記錄。
vocabulary.example: The footage captured by the diver provided scientists with invaluable data about the shark's behaviour and size.

vocabulary.definition: The careful protection and preservation of the natural environment and wildlife from damage or destruction.
vocabulary.definition_zh: 對自然環境和野生動物進行謹慎的保護與維護，防止其受到破壞或毀滅。
vocabulary.example: Marine conservation efforts in the Mediterranean have intensified following the rare sighting of the Great White shark.

vocabulary.definition: An occasion when something rare or unusual is seen, especially an animal that is hard to find in the wild.
vocabulary.definition_zh: 目擊罕見或不尋常事物的場合，尤指在野外難以發現的動物。
vocabulary.example: The confirmed sighting of a Great White shark in the Mediterranean was immediately reported to marine research institutes.

questions.q: How did the diver feel when he encountered the Great White shark?
options: A: He felt completely calm and professional throughout the encounter.
B: He was shaking, suggesting a combination of fear and excitement.
C: He was so frightened that he immediately swam back to the surface.
D: He felt confident because he had encountered Great Whites before.
questions.explanation: The transcript states that the diver was 'shaking with a mixture of fear and awe,' indicating he was both frightened and in awe, not calm or immediately fleeing.

questions.q: Why is the Great White shark sighting in the Mediterranean considered scientifically significant?
options: A: Because the shark was unusually large, measuring over ten metres.
B: Because it was the first time a diver had ever filmed a shark underwater.
C: Because Great White sharks have been largely absent from Mediterranean waters for decades.
D: Because the footage revealed a previously unknown species of shark.
questions.explanation: The transcript clearly states that 'Great White sharks have been virtually absent from Mediterranean waters for decades,' making any confirmed sighting scientifically remarkable.

questions.q: What do conservation groups hope the viral footage will achieve?
options: A: Encourage more divers to seek out dangerous marine wildlife.
B: Raise public awareness and pressure governments to protect Great White sharks.
C: Prove that the Mediterranean is no longer a safe place for swimming.
D: Attract more tourism to the waters between Tunisia and Sicily.
questions.explanation: The transcript states that conservation groups hope the footage 'will raise public awareness and put pressure on governments to take more decisive action to safeguard these apex predators.'

reading.title: The Plight of the Great White Shark: Conservation Challenges in the Mediterranean Sea
reading.passage:
The Great White shark (Carcharodon carcharias) has long captured the human imagination, yet this iconic marine predator faces an increasingly uncertain future. Recent rare footage of a Great White shark filmed in the Sicilian Channel, between Tunisia and Sicily, has reignited global debate about the conservation status of the species in the Mediterranean Sea — one of the world's most heavily fished and ecologically stressed bodies of water.

Historically, the Mediterranean was home to a viable population of Great White sharks. Historical records and fisher testimonies suggest that sightings were relatively common as recently as the mid-twentieth century. However, decades of intensive commercial fishing, including bycatch — the unintentional capture of non-target species — combined with the deliberate targeting of sharks for their fins and liver oil, has led to a catastrophic decline in numbers. Current estimates suggest that fewer than fifty individual Great White sharks may remain in Mediterranean waters, though accurate population data is notoriously difficult to obtain.

The species is listed as Vulnerable on the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) Red List and receives nominal protection under several international agreements, including the Barcelona Convention. Nevertheless, enforcement of these protections is widely regarded as inadequate. Illegal fishing continues in protected zones, and the political will to impose meaningful penalties on violators remains weak across many Mediterranean nations.

Marine biologists argue that the recovery of Great White shark populations is not only desirable from a biodiversity standpoint but is ecologically essential. As apex predators, Great Whites regulate the populations of species lower in the food chain, thereby maintaining the structural integrity of marine ecosystems. The loss of such a keystone species could trigger cascading ecological consequences that would ultimately undermine the productivity of Mediterranean fisheries themselves.

The recent viral footage, therefore, represents more than a thrilling wildlife encounter. It is a stark reminder of how close to the edge of extinction this magnificent species has come, and a call to action for policymakers, scientists, and the public alike.

questions.statement: Great White sharks were commonly sighted in the Mediterranean Sea during the middle of the twentieth century.
questions.explanation: The passage states that 'sightings were relatively common as recently as the mid-twentieth century,' directly supporting this statement as True.

questions.statement: Scientists have been able to precisely determine the current Great White shark population in the Mediterranean.
questions.explanation: The passage states that 'accurate population data is notoriously difficult to obtain,' indicating that precise population figures are not available, making this statement False.

questions.statement: The Barcelona Convention offers greater protection to Great White sharks than any other international agreement worldwide.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions the Barcelona Convention as one of the agreements offering nominal protection, but makes no comparison with other international agreements globally, so this cannot be determined.

questions.q: According to the passage, why is the recovery of Great White shark populations considered ecologically essential?
options: A: Because Great White sharks attract marine tourism and boost coastal economies.
B: Because as apex predators, they regulate other species and maintain ecosystem balance.
C: Because their liver oil is a valuable resource for the pharmaceutical industry.
D: Because their presence deters illegal fishing activity in protected marine zones.
questions.explanation: The passage explicitly states that 'Great Whites regulate the populations of species lower in the food chain, thereby maintaining the structural integrity of marine ecosystems,' making option B correct.

speaking.topic: Describe a wildlife or nature experience that had a strong impact on you.
speaking.topic_zh: 描述一次對你產生深刻影響的野生動物或自然體驗。

part2.cue_card:
Describe a wildlife or nature experience that had a strong impact on you.

You should say:
• What the experience was and where it took place
• What you saw or encountered during the experience
• How you felt at the time and immediately afterwards

And explain why this experience had such a strong and lasting impact on you.
band_tips: Use a wide range of emotional and sensory vocabulary to vividly describe the encounter — words like 'awe-inspiring,' 'humbling,' 'visceral,' and 'overwhelming' signal lexical sophistication to the examiner.

Demonstrate grammatical range by mixing narrative tenses (past simple, past continuous, past perfect) with reflective language ('Looking back, I realise...', 'What struck me most was...') to show both accuracy and flexibility.

Extend your response naturally by connecting the personal experience to broader themes such as environmental awareness, the fragility of nature, or a shift in your perspective — this shows the depth of thinking expected at Band 8-9.

part3.q: Why do you think wildlife encounters, like filming a rare shark, often go viral on social media?
part3.q_zh: 你認為為什麼與野生動物的相遇，例如拍攝稀有鯊魚，往往在社群媒體上瘋傳？
key_points: Humans are instinctively drawn to rare or dangerous animals due to a combination of fear and fascination, making such content inherently shareable.
Social media algorithms prioritise emotionally engaging content, and wildlife encounters trigger strong reactions ranging from wonder to concern.
Viral wildlife footage can serve as a form of informal environmental education, drawing attention to species that many people were unaware were endangered.

part3.q: Do you think governments are doing enough to protect endangered marine species? Why or why not?
part3.q_zh: 你認為政府在保護瀕危海洋物種方面做得足夠嗎？為什麼？
key_points: While international agreements and protected marine zones exist on paper, enforcement is frequently inadequate due to limited resources and weak political will.
Economic pressures, particularly in fishing-dependent coastal communities, often lead governments to prioritise short-term industry interests over long-term ecological sustainability.
A more effective approach would involve a combination of stricter penalties for illegal fishing, increased funding for marine research, and cross-border cooperation among Mediterranean nations.

part3.q: How can ordinary people contribute to the conservation of endangered animals like the Great White shark?
part3.q_zh: 普通人如何能為保護大白鯊等瀕危動物做出貢獻？
key_points: Consumers can make informed choices by avoiding seafood products associated with unsustainable or destructive fishing practices, thereby reducing market demand.
Supporting reputable conservation organisations financially or through volunteer activities, such as citizen science projects, can significantly amplify research and advocacy efforts.
Sharing verified, scientifically accurate information about endangered species on social media can build public pressure on policymakers to enact stronger protective legislation.

part3.q: Some people argue that the fear surrounding sharks is exaggerated and damages conservation efforts. Do you agree?
part3.q_zh: 有些人認為對鯊魚的恐懼被誇大了，並且損害了保育工作。你同意嗎？
key_points: Popular culture, particularly films like Jaws, has deeply distorted public perception of sharks, portraying them as mindless killers rather than ecologically vital animals that rarely interact aggressively with humans.

This irrational fear has historically made it politically easier for governments to deprioritise shark conservation, as the public is unlikely to advocate for protecting animals they perceive as threats.

Reframing the narrative through education and accurate media representation is crucial — when people understand that sharks are far more endangered by humans than vice versa, conservation support tends to increase significantly.

writing.task2_question: Some people believe that protecting endangered marine species, such as sharks, should be a top priority for governments worldwide. Others, however, argue that addressing human poverty and economic development is far more urgent. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
writing.task2_question_zh: 有些人認為保護瀕危海洋物種（如鯊魚）應是全球各國政府的首要任務。然而，也有人認為解決人類貧困與促進經濟發展更為迫切。討論兩種觀點並給出你自己的意見。

useful_phrases.when_to_use: Use to introduce a widely accepted fact or premise before presenting a contrasting or nuanced argument.
useful_phrases.when_to_use_zh: 用於在提出對比或細緻的論點之前，引入一個被廣泛接受的事實或前提。
useful_phrases.example: It is undeniable that addressing acute human poverty demands immediate governmental attention; however, this should not preclude simultaneous investment in ecological preservation.

useful_phrases.when_to_use: Use to introduce an argument grounded in environmental science, signalling an academic and analytical perspective.
useful_phrases.when_to_use_zh: 用於引入基於環境科學的論點，展現學術性和分析性的視角。
useful_phrases.example: From an ecological standpoint, the extinction of apex predators like the Great White shark would trigger irreversible damage to marine food webs with long-term consequences for human food security.

useful_phrases.when_to_use: Use when arguing that two issues presented as opposing choices are not actually mutually exclusive, demonstrating critical thinking.
useful_phrases.when_to_use_zh: 當論證兩個被呈現為對立選擇的議題實際上並非相互排斥時使用，展現批判性思維。
useful_phrases.example: A false dichotomy exists between conservation and development; well-managed marine ecosystems can, in fact, sustain fishing industries and coastal livelihoods over the long term.

useful_phrases.phrase: The long-term ramifications of... cannot be overstated.
useful_phrases.when_to_use: Use to emphasise the serious and far-reaching consequences of a particular issue or inaction, particularly in conclusion or topic sentences.
useful_phrases.when_to_use_zh: 用於強調某一特定問題或不作為所帶來的嚴重且深遠的後果，尤其適用於結論或主題句。
useful_phrases.example: The long-term ramifications of allowing endangered marine species to disappear cannot be overstated, as biodiversity loss ultimately threatens the stability of ecosystems upon which billions of people depend.

useful_phrases.phrase: Proponents of this view contend that...
useful_phrases.when_to_use: Use in a discussion essay to fairly and accurately represent a perspective that you may not personally hold, before offering a counter-argument.
useful_phrases.when_to_use_zh: 在討論型作文中，用於公平且準確地呈現你可能不認同的觀點，然後再提出反駁論點。
useful_phrases.example: Proponents of this view contend that diverting public funds towards species conservation is a moral luxury that developing nations simply cannot afford.

outline.intro: Open with a thought-provoking statement about the tension between environmental and human needs (e.g., referencing the rare shark sighting as a symbol of this dilemma). Paraphrase the question by acknowledging that both issues are genuinely important, then state your position clearly — that the two goals are not mutually exclusive and that a balanced, integrated approach is both possible and necessary.
outline.body1: Present the argument in favour of prioritising human poverty and economic development. Acknowledge that millions of people lack access to clean water, food, and healthcare, and that governments have a fundamental duty to address these immediate human needs. Note that fishing communities in regions like the Mediterranean depend economically on marine resources and that overly restrictive conservation policies can deepen poverty if implemented without adequate social support systems.
outline.body2: Argue that protecting endangered marine species is equally essential and directly connected to long-term human welfare. Explain that apex predators like the Great White shark are keystone species whose disappearance would destabilise marine ecosystems, ultimately reducing fish stocks and threatening the food security of billions. Emphasise that conservation and development are complementary rather than competing priorities — sustainable fisheries management, for example, serves both ecological and economic goals simultaneously.
outline.conclusion: Restate your position that the framing of this issue as an either/or choice is misleading. Governments should pursue integrated policies that combine poverty alleviation with ecological stewardship, recognising that a healthy natural environment is the foundation upon which long-term human prosperity depends. End with a forward-looking statement about the responsibility of the current generation to preserve both human dignity and natural heritage.
